title: What Is Your Gut Microbiome?
description: Expert medical guidance from Christine Lee, MD
author: Christine Lee, MD
trust_explanation: STRENGTHS: Expert/Trusted Author | High-Authority Domain | Scientifically Referenced || WEAKNESSES: None noted.
content_chunks: Your gut microbiome is a microscopic world within the world of your larger body. The trillions of microorganisms that live there affect each other and their environment in various ways.
Microorganisms living inside your gut — inside your intestines — make up your gut microbiome, populateed by trillions of microscopic organisms.
Bacteria in your gut help break down certain complex carbohydrates and dietary fibers that you can’t break down on your own.

title: How Does Your Gut Microbiome Affect Your Health?
description: Expert medical guidance from Healthline Medical Team
author: Healthline Medical Team
trust_explanation: STRENGTHS: Expert/Trusted Author | High-Authority Domain | Scientifically Referenced | Strong Medical Disclaimer || WEAKNESSES: None noted.
content_chunks: Microorganisms, or microbes, include bacteria, viruses, fungi, and other microscopic living things. Trillions of these exist inside your intestines.
While some bacteria are associated with disease, others are crucial for your immune system, heart, weight, and overall health.
Humans have evolved to live with microbes over millions of years. New evidence suggests higher microbiome diversity is considered good for your health.

title: Everything you need to know about the gut microbiome
description: Expert medical guidance from Medical News Today
author: Medical News Today
trust_explanation: STRENGTHS: Expert/Trusted Author | Scientifically Referenced | Strong Medical Disclaimer || WEAKNESSES: Non-specialized domain
content_chunks: Protein powders are nutritional supplements that may help build muscle, repair tissue, and make enzymes and hormones.
Using protein powder may also aid weight loss and help people tone their muscles.
There are many different types of protein powder, including dairy-based and plant-based powders.

author: TED-Ed
trust_explanation: STRENGTHS: Expert/Trusted Author || WEAKNESSES: Non-specialized domain | Outdated information
content_chunks: The bacteria in our guts can break down food the body can’t digest, produce important nutrients, and regulate the immune system.
We can manipulate the balance of our microbes by paying attention to what we eat.
How the food you eat affects your gut - Shilpa Ravella.

author: Kurzgesagt – In a Nutshell
trust_explanation: STRENGTHS: Expert/Trusted Author || WEAKNESSES: Non-specialized domain | Outdated information
content_chunks: What happens when microbes talk to your brain?
Trillions of bacteria, viruses, and fungi live inside you, collectively known as the microbiome.
How Bacteria Rule Over Your Body – The Microbiome.

author: Makki K, Deehan EC, Walter J, Bäckhed F
trust_explanation: STRENGTHS: High-Authority Domain | Scientifically Referenced || WEAKNESSES: None noted.
content_chunks: The Impact of Dietary Fiber on Gut Microbiota in Host Health and Disease.
Diet is not only essential to maintain human growth but also modulates and supports the microbial communities.
Type, quality, and origin of our food shape our gut microbes and affect their composition and function.
